what_input_feature: A glyph buffer routed to HB_SCRIPT_DEVANAGARI whose current codepoint is 0x0905 and whose next codepoint (buffer->cur(1)) equals one of 0x0946/0x0949/0x094A/0x094B, so the inner switch on cur(1).codepoint takes the true arm at line 68.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile wins 8/10 and reaches the branch true side (avg hitcount 9 vs 0); its CMP_MAP gradient steadily preserves inputs whose CMP operands inch toward the required Devanagari codepoint constants, and the resolving seed carries 09 09 05 09 byte runs consistent with the 0x0905/0x094X 16-bit codepoints driving the switch.
why_loser_doesnt: value_profile_cmplog resolves only 2/10 (avg hitcount 0); it pays I2SRandReplace per-execution cost and corpus churn on top of the same CMP_MAP gradient, and its blocking seeds stay on the false arm (line 68 L counts T=0/T=1 F=17/F=18) without assembling the 0x0905-then-0x094X pair.
mechanism_attribution: Both involved fuzzers share the CMP_MAP gradient (Hamming/prefix distance feedback) that incrementally rewards inputs whose codepoint CMP operands approach the case constants; value_profile beats value_profile_cmplog because the winner relies on that gradient unburdened, whereas adding the I2S input-to-state substitution mutator (the single technique delta) does not help solve this nested 16-bit-codepoint switch and its overhead/corpus churn dilutes the loser's progress.
